summary: Ground-system decomp matching PR touching Castle, Great Bay, Ice Mountain, and Venom source files in the final changed-file metadata. The PR body says exact-match decompilation of 5 files and 4 newly matched functions, while final changed-file metadata lists 4 files; review discussion also references grKongo changes that the author says were removed. Evidence supports final code changes around loop cleanup, register/stack-sensitive local ordering, accessor choice, assert cleanup, and avoiding data-order-driven literal/global substitutions.

key_files.role: Final changed file; Castle functions adjusted for matching, including natural loop initialization, platform state update codegen, local declaration/PAD_STACK changes, GET_JOBJ placement, and compound assignment cleanup.
Final changed file; small register-allocation-oriented temporary added around gobj->hsd_obj before Ground_801C2ED0.

Final changed file; Ice Mountain matching cleanup, including declaration ordering, converting a do-while to for-loop, keeping HSD_ASSERTs, replacing noinline helper calls, and using HSD_GObjGetHSDObj where GET_JOBJ did not match.
Final changed file; Venom matching improvements using stack padding, temporary locals, ternary assignment, declaration initialization, and assignment-in-condition shaping.
Reviewed but not listed in final changed-file metadata; review comments describe removed or rejected changes involving extern string substitution and sdata2 ordering.

what_changed: PR body claims exact-match decompilation of 5 files and 4 newly matched functions, with slice verification by ninja changes_all and clean regression report.
Final changed-file metadata lists 4 files: grcastle.c, grgreatbay.c, gricemt.c, and grvenom.c.
Bot report in loaded text corpus reported 2 new matches in this slice: grCastle_801CD658 and grVenom_80204284, plus 9 improvements in unmatched items.
grCastle_801CD658 replaced eight repeated dynamics[i].data = NULL assignments plus a later loop with a single loop from i = 0 to 12.
grIceMt fn_801F8E58 was converted to for (i = 0; i < 12; i++), while retaining walking Ground* and s32* pointers for matching.
grIceMt_801FA500 ultimately used HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(arg0), after review questioned a one-off helper and suggested GET_JOBJ.
grIceMt_801F9ACC final diff shows HSD_ASSERT calls retained for mgobj/jobj assertions in the visible changed areas.
grVenom_80203B18 added stack padding and temporary locals, used a ternary for flag assignment, introduced diff and zero temporaries, and used assignment within an lobj null check.
grVenom_80204284 added a tmp_gp temporary before assigning gp, matching the bot-reported newly matched function.
grKongo changes discussed in review are not present in final changed-file metadata; author stated one grKongo issue was removed because it could not be fully resolved.

smart_moves.text: Accepted reviewer direction to make the Ice Mountain candidate loop an explicit for-loop over 0..11, while preserving walking pointers where indexing broke register allocation.
Dropped a one-off HSD_JObj helper in favor of an existing accessor call, but documented why GET_JOBJ itself did not match in this context.
Removed or did not ship grKongo changes that reviewers identified as regressions around extern string usage and sdata2 ordering.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Natural counted loops are preferred when repeated blocks differ only by index; in this PR, Castle initialization was collapsed into a 0..12 loop and Ice Mountain review requested a for-loop over 0..12.
A loop can be source-cleaner while still keeping pointer-walking temporaries if direct indexing changes MWCC register allocation; record the exact reason and byte-identical evidence.
Keep HSD_ASSERT forms when the source-level assert macro is already present; expanding or replacing it with raw __assert-style code was called a regression in review.
Do not replace ordinary literals or assert strings with extern/global symbols just to influence sdata2 or data ordering; reviewers labeled such grKongo changes regressions.

naming_conventions.name: jobj_tmp
naming_conventions.convention: Temporary names may be used for register-allocation-sensitive intermediate values when the role is clear but the temporary is not semantically meaningful.

naming_conventions.name: tmp_gp
naming_conventions.convention: A temporary gp alias can be introduced for matching when it simply stages GET_GROUND(gobj) before the real gp assignment.

naming_conventions.name: speed_cap, max_val, min_val, diff, zero
naming_conventions.convention: Short role-based locals were used for arithmetic bounds and repeated values rather than inventing broader semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK narrowly where final diff shows stack-shape steering in grCastle_801CDC44 and grVenom_80203B18.
Keep declaration order and early initialization when it affects register allocation; grVenom_80206874 moved zero/base/gp/jobj/scale initialization into declarations.
Use existing accessor HSD_GObjGetHSDObj when raw gobj->hsd_obj is less desirable, but avoid GET_JOBJ if its explicit cast changes MWCC stack/register allocation.
Ternary assignment can replace small branch ladders when it preserves the intended source shape and match; grVenom_80203B18 used flag = condition ? flag : 0.
Assignment inside a null-check was used for lobj in grVenom_80203B18, likely for code shape; evidence only proves the final source form, not a general preference.

review_feedback.text: Regression: keep the original HSD_ASSERT in grIceMt_801F9ACC instead of converting to a raw null-check/assert expansion.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

review_feedback.text: Reviewer noted the unresolved assert issue: marked resolved but still using raw ___asserts.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.text: Try turning Ice Mountain candidate collection into a for-loop from 0 to 12.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.text: Questioned whether grIceMt_801FA500 should use GET_JOBJ; author resolved by using HSD_GObjGetHSDObj directly and explaining why GET_JOBJ did not byte-match.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle/fjooord

review_feedback.text: Do not change grKongo assert string usage to an extern char; reviewer said it was already declared and should be removed elsewhere instead.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

review_feedback.text: Reviewer called a grKongo rewrite a regression because it was trying to fix sdata2 ordering.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

follow_up_queries: PR 2657 grIceMt_801FA500 GET_JOBJ HSD_GObjGetHSDObj frame grows 32 40
PR 2657 fn_801F8E58 for loop walking Ground pointer s32 p byte-identical
PR 2657 grKongo sdata2 ordering extern char regression removed
PR 2657 grCastle_801CD658 natural loop dynamics data NULL

notes: Strong evidence for final diffs in 4 files listed by changed_files metadata.
PR body says 5 files and includes grkongo.c, but final changed_files metadata omits grkongo.c; review comments and author response indicate at least some grKongo edits were removed.

PR body claims 4 newly matched functions, while loaded bot report excerpt says 2 new matches and 9 improvements for the visible report. This record does not reconcile that discrepancy beyond preserving both evidence points.
Author comments provide byte-identical/object evidence for two Ice Mountain review resolutions; broader verification is from PR body and bot excerpt, not independently rerun.
Several matching tactics are visible in final diff, but exact necessity is only explicitly documented for the Ice Mountain for-loop pointer shape and HSD_GObjGetHSDObj vs GET_JOBJ case.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer natural loops, but keep pointer-walking when indexing breaks the match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_801F8E58, review requested a for-loop over i = 0..11. The author converted to for (i = 0; i < 12; i++) but kept walking Ground* g and s32* p pointers because indexing (&gp->gv.icemt.xDC)[i] shifted register allocation and broke the match; author reported byte-identical output.

accepted_candidate_records.title: GET_JOBJ can be too strong when its cast changes MWCC codegen
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grIceMt_801FA500, review suggested GET_JOBJ. The final code used HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(arg0) directly. The author reported GET_JOBJ's explicit HSD_JObj* cast on the inline void* result created an extra stack temp, grew the frame from 32 to 40, and reshuffled registers; the bare inline call with implicit pointer conversion was byte-identical.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not convert existing HSD_ASSERTs to raw assert forms during matching cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewers called replacing an HSD_ASSERT in grIceMt_801F9ACC with raw assert/null-check style a regression and asked to keep the original HSD_ASSERT.

source_update_candidates.title: Document GET_JOBJ exception when cast changes MWCC frame/register allocation
source_update_candidates.text: Header/accessor cleanup should prefer canonical helpers, but PR 2657 shows a narrow exception: HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(arg0) may match where GET_JOBJ does not, because GET_JOBJ's explicit HSD_JObj* cast on the inline void* result can introduce an extra stack temp and register reshuffle.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing header-inlines standard cautions that GET_JOBJ is not unconditional cleanup; this PR gives concrete reviewer-tested wording for the exception.

source_update_candidates.title: Explicitly discourage literal-to-extern substitutions for sdata2 ordering
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewers rejected grKongo changes that replaced literal/assert values with extern data symbols and said the changes were regressions attempting to fix sdata2 ordering.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards already cover this pattern; this PR can serve as supporting historical evidence if the curator wants to add a concrete sdata2-ordering example.

source_update_candidates.title: Ground stage files use HSD_ASSERT and HSD/Ground accessors in matching-sensitive code
source_update_candidates.text: In src/melee/gr/gricemt.c review pushed back on raw assert expansion and on a one-off jobj helper, resulting in retained HSD_ASSERTs and use of HSD_GObjGetHSDObj.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path-specific style fact; should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote grKongo as a final changed file for this PR slice; final changed_files metadata omits it despite PR body and review references.
Do not infer that GET_JOBJ is generally bad; evidence only supports one Ice Mountain case where the macro's cast changed codegen.
Do not accept PAD_STACK or extra dummy temporaries as general style from this PR; final diff shows them, but only some tactics have explicit byte-identical justification.
Do not claim all 4 newly matched functions by name from loaded evidence; bot excerpt names 2 new matches while PR body gives a higher count.
